sources.notes: Authoritative CKC work status and current focus.

hard_rejections: SQLite forbidden in any form for Handshake.
Electron is not authority for Handshake architecture.
Localhost intake is not authority for Handshake integration design.
CKC namespace must migrate to Handshake-owned Atelier/Lens vocabulary.

capability_clusters.name: Image-first library and structured authoring surface
requirements: Images are the hero surface beside structured sheet/editor content.
Global and per-entity carousel/photo modes exist.
Controls, filters, and command bars are minimal or hidden by default.
Ratings and filters affect slideshow/gallery behavior.

capability_clusters.name: Intake, Inbox, and triage
requirements: Persistent intake batches.
Accepted, pending, and rejected lanes.
Linked or loose classification.
Optional linkage to structured records, versions, and collections.
Pending Inbox lifecycle and filesystem health checks.
handshake_overlap: Lens canonical import and triage lane.
Atelier delayed assignment of assets to subject/project/version records.
Agent-readable batch audit and recovery state.

capability_clusters.name: Collections, playlists, and contact sheets
requirements: Curated cross-entity image sets.
Collection notes, tags, optional links, batch tag application.
SVG plus manifest contact sheets with source IDs, hashes, tags, and layout metadata.
Raster contact sheet export remains planned.
handshake_overlap: Lens review sets and visual comparison sheets.
Atelier planning boards and shot/reference collections.

capability_clusters.name: Pose, identity reference, sidecars, and workflow replay
requirements: Blank, single-photo, and collection pose workbench contexts.
Deterministic no-space derivative sidecar paths.
Body, face, hand, and head-pose control metadata.
Identity/reference profile crops and downstream bridge payloads.
Workflow replay can inject identity/reference payloads.
handshake_overlap: Production workflow provenance and replay concepts.
cautions: CKC localhost ComfyUI bridge is not authority.
Deep calibration/history UX is blocked in CKC.

capability_clusters.name: LLM automation, visual debugging, and parallel operation
requirements: Manual-command-dispatch consistency is tested.
State inspection, command execution, screenshot capture, sessions, leases, and logs are exposed.
Background automation must not steal focus.
Parallel editing uses PostgreSQL source of truth, leases, optimistic revisions, append-only events, and limited safe-shape CRDT merges.

capability_clusters.name: Storage, portability, and governance/product split
requirements: PostgreSQL is the default/current CKC provider for new runs.
No-space generated artifact paths.
Governance and product output are separate.
SQLite remains in CKC only as legacy/test fallback, which Handshake rejects.

data_entities.meaning: Image file plus metadata, tags, ratings, hashes, palette/dHash, provenance, and sidecar role.
Structured subject record with sheet fields, public ID, icon, and images.
Structured field values with versioning, validation, diff, merge, and revert.
Separate document and canvas libraries with smart tags.
Curated image sets/playlists with export and slideshow behavior.
Import, triage, dedup, acceptance, pending, and rejection audit records.
Pose/control representation derived from source images.
Deterministic reference crop plus landmarks, measurements, pose metadata, and bridge payload.
Workflow provenance, prompt/story metadata, and replay surfaces.
Parallel model/operator coordination and audit state.
data_entities.handshake_candidate: Agent session, operation lease, event journal, revision ledger

data_entities.meaning: Generated visual artifact manifest with source IDs, hashes, tags, and layout metadata.

evolved_features.handshake_value: Improves Lens/Atelier operator speed and reduces visual clutter.
Makes asset capture cheap while preserving a canonical triage path.
High ROI for large Lens libraries and repeated Atelier cleanup work.
Atelier can reuse the capability shape for production boards if moodboards are in scope.
Reduces data loss and helps agents recover without guessing.
Directly supports parallel LLM operation and reproducible validation.

overlap_candidates.base_scope: Persistent asset intake and triage lane.
handshake_changes: Use Handshake storage rules with no SQLite

overlap_candidates.base_scope: Curated review sets and manifest-backed contact sheets.
Side-by-side references and structured editable profile/spec data.
handshake_changes: Replace character-sheet taxonomy with Atelier domain model
Prevent governance leakage into product UI

overlap_candidates.base_scope: Source assets linked to generated/derived sidecars.
handshake_changes: Map Pose/OpenPose only if Lens owns pose/control outputs

overlap_candidates.base_scope: Manual, command map, automation state, leases, logs, and visual verification.
handshake_changes: Do not copy CKC names or product paths

conflicts.ckc_state: CKC retains SQLite as legacy/test fallback and has planned SQLite removal/quarantine.
conflicts.mitigation: Do not import CKC SQLite references into Handshake architecture, specs, tests, examples, or fixtures.

conflicts.ckc_state: CKC uses Electron contextBridge, nativeImage, webContents capture, and electron-builder.
conflicts.handshake_decision: Treat Electron as CKC implementation detail only.
conflicts.mitigation: Reuse behavior constraints such as non-focus-stealing capture, not Electron APIs as requirements.

conflicts.ckc_state: CKC has localhost-only ComfyUI intake/server behavior with bearer token support.
conflicts.handshake_decision: Do not make localhost intake canonical.
conflicts.mitigation: Redesign integration around Handshake-owned transport, auth, provenance, and replay contracts.

conflicts.ckc_state: CKC names include CastKit Codex, CKC, CKC_GOV, CKC_main, PoseKit, Rig, Workflow, and CastKitCodexBridge.
conflicts.handshake_decision: Migrate all CKC names into Handshake Atelier/Lens vocabulary.
conflicts.mitigation: Create explicit name mapping before authoring Handshake specs or taskboard rows.

conflicts.ckc_state: CKC governance lives in CKC_GOV and stale product doc mirrors were removed.
conflicts.handshake_decision: .GOV is governance/reference only, not product output.
conflicts.mitigation: Keep generated product docs, exports, and UI help separate from .GOV paths and labels.

items: WP-0001..WP-0115 mostly complete, including portfolio, media metadata, moodboards, exports, search, collections, PostgreSQL storage, automation, intake, PoseKit, workflow replay, identity profiles, hand detection, and multi-rig tabs.
WP-0122..WP-0132, WP-0134..WP-0135, and WP-0137 are in review.
WP-0137 fixed product/manual dispatch drift, PostgreSQL-compatible regression coverage, and full npm test pass.
WP-0133 PoseKit calibration, markers, and history is blocked by deeper interaction needs.
WP-0117 stylized portrait landmark detector research.
WP-0119 PostgreSQL-first testing and SQLite removal.
WP-0118 model prompt-response matrix is parked as future brainstorming, not current work.

unresolved_work.handshake_implication: Start stricter than CKC: no SQLite fallback or fixture dependence.
Adopt SVG/manifest first; add raster only if Lens needs it.
Do not assume deep pose-edit UX is solved.
Treat multi-subject control as future scope unless explicitly required.
Reuse the research shape, but run a current Handshake-specific field pass.

notes: This draft is source-grounded extraction, not a final Handshake spec.
CKC REVIEW rows should be treated as implemented but not fully accepted until review evidence is accepted.
Behavioral patterns are more reusable than CKC code names or implementation stack.
